issues.sample: As I was driving home with about 5 minutes to go, in my brand new (2 week old)2023 Ford Escape, I smelled something burning. I pulled over and saw smoke so I turned off the car and got out. As I got out of the car I looked under and saw fire. I got away from the car and as I did,

3 times, either merging on the highway or full throttle, vehicle screams overheat warning with wrench light for a couple of seconds and disappears. No limp mode or smoke at all. And coolant level is at MAX. No clue is this some sort of glitch or engine failure? Vehicles drives am

narrative.headline: What 2023 Ford Escape owners report most
narrative.summary: Across 89 owner complaints to NHTSA, the most reported area is Electrical System (42% of reports). Some reports involve a fire or crash — read the details below.
